title: cacalia fulta
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", Cacalia fulta may have immunoenhancing properties due to its ability to stimulate the production of anti-inflammatory interleukin-10 (IL-10) and possess immunostimulatory activities in comparison with levamisole.
parts: Cacalia fulta has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and flowers. The leaves of Cacalia fulta are known to contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The roots of the plant are rich in sesquiterpenoids, including compounds like cacalol and fultanol, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities. Additionally, the flowers of Cacalia fulta contain essential oils, including sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, which have been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Cacalia fulta can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and swelling. Overconsumption of the plant's extracts can also lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps. Additionally, it may cause interactions with certain medications, leading to adverse effects on blood pressure and other cardiovascular health. In severe cases, improper use of Cacalia fulta can result in more serious conditions, such as liver damage and respiratory failure.
